message:
perf(btree): gate cell-slot cache hit/miss counters behind copy-profile flag

The bd-9e3xf classification counters (CELL_SLOT_CACHE_HITS,
_MISS_COLD, _MISS_INVALIDATED, _MISS_SLOT) were incremented
unconditionally on every `CellSlotCache::get`. On the 1-thread write
hot path those `AtomicU64::fetch_add(1, Relaxed)` fires are pure
observability cost; every btree op that traverses the cache pays them.
Under MT they also create cross-core cache-line contention on the
shared global statics.

Every other btree-instrumentation counter that only exists for
observability (record_local_payload_copy, record_owned_payload_
materialization, record_overflow_reassembly, record_table_leaf_cell_
assembly, record_index_leaf_cell_assembly, record_interior_cell_
rebuild, record_fast_table_leaf_payload_append_*, …) already gates
itself behind `copy_profile_enabled()`. Bring the cell-slot counters
onto that same path:

- Expose `instrumentation::copy_profile_enabled()` as `pub(crate)`.
- Wrap the six `fetch_add` call sites inside `CellSlotCache::get` /
  `get_slow` with `if copy_profile_enabled() { … }`.
- Make the `audit_cell_slot_cache_hit_rate_write_vs_read` bench flip
  the flag on (and restore on Drop) so it still observes the counts.

Measured overhead on the 1-thread hot-front-entry `get` path
(new bench `bench_cell_slot_cache_get_gate_on_vs_off`, release, prefill
32 entries, 10 M iters):
  profile_off = 4.162 ns / call  (production default, 1t write path)
  profile_on  = 4.198 ns / call  (observability / audit mode)
  counter_cost = 0.036 ns / call

The 1t delta itself is small — Relaxed fetch_add is nearly free when
the cache line stays in L1 exclusive. The structural value is avoiding
global-static contention on MT workloads where hundreds of writer
threads would otherwise all be touching the same four cache lines for
counters nobody reads in production.

Parity: cargo check -p fsqlite-btree --tests clean; 364/365 lib tests
        pass single-threaded (the one failure is the long-standing
        pre-existing prop_index_btree_structural_invariants flake on
        clean HEAD). Audit bench still produces the same classification
        output because the test enables the gate before running.
Bead:   bd-9e3xf (counters were introduced there; this is the
                  follow-up that makes them zero-cost in production).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
